title: Device and method for additive casting of parts
abstract: A method and an apparatus for additive casting of parts is disclosed. The method may include: depositing, on a build table, a first portion of a mold, such that, the depositing may be performed layer by layer; pouring liquid substance into the first portion of the mold to form a first casted layer; solidifying at least a portion of the first casted layer; depositing a second portion of the mold, on top of the first portion of the mold; pouring the liquid substance into the second portion of the mold to form a second casted layer, on top of at least a portion of the first casted layer; and solidifying at least a portion of the second casted layer. The method may further include joining the first and second casted layers prior to the pouring of a third casted layer.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  [0096] What is claimed is: 1. A method of additive casting of parts, comprising: depositing, on a build table, a first portion of a mold, wherein the depositing is performed layer by layer; pouring liquid substance into the first portion of the mold to form a first casted layer; solidifying at least a portion of the first casted layer; depositing a second portion of the mold, on top of the first portion of the mold; pouring the liquid substance into the second portion of the mold to form a second casted layer, on top of at least a portion of the first casted layer; and solidifying at least a portion of the second casted layer. 2. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: receiving a three-dimensional (3D) part model comprising one or more parts, the part model is divided into a plurality of casted layers. 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising: receiving a 3D mold model, the mold model is divided into a plurality of mold portions, wherein the mold model is design to provide a desired shape to the liquid substance. 4. The method of claim 2, further comprising: generating a 3D mold model based on the received part model, the mold model is divided into a plurality of mold portions, wherein the mold model is design to provide a designed shape to a liquid substance. 5. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the liquid substance is one of: a molten metal, a molten glass and a polymer resin. 6. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: joining the first and second casted layers prior to the pouring of a third casted layer. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein joining comprises melting at least a portion of the interface between the first and second casted layers. 8. The method of claim 6 or claim 7, wherein joining comprises treating at least a portion of an upper surface of the second casted layer with at least one of: an induction heater, a resistance welder, an ultrasonic welder, plasma deposition unit, E-beam, a laser, a welding arc, a torch, cold fusion and magnetic field flow. 9. The method of claim 6, wherein joining comprises at least one of: gluing, ultrasonic bonding, diffusion bonding, heat curing and ultraviolet (UV) curing. 10. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: pre-heating each casted layer prior to the pouring of an additional casted layer. 11. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: providing surface treatment to each casted layer after solidification and prior to the pouring of an additional casted layer. 12. The method of claim 1 1, wherein the surface treatment comprises at least one of: machining, grinding, polishing and laser ablation. 13. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: providing surface treatment to internal walls of each mold portion prior to the pouring of the corresponding casted layer. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the surface treatment to internal walls of each mold portion comprises machining the internal walls and removing the excess material. 15. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising leveling the first casted layer and the first mold portion to be in the same level prior to the deposition of the second mold portion. 16. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the depositing and pouring steps are performed under a protective atmosphere. 17. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first and second casting layers have different thicknesses. 18. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first and second casted layers have thickness of between 0.1-12 mm. 19. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein each mold layer comprises a mixture of granular material and a binder, wherein the granular material may include at least one of: ceramic powders, sand, clay, and any combination thereof. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the mixture further comprises metallic powder. 21. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein depositing the one or more mold layers comprises printing each mold layer using a 3D printer. 22. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein pouring the liquid substance is from a movable pouring unit comprising at least one liquid introduction port for pouring liquid substance. 23. The method of claim 16, wherein the movable pouring unit is configured to pour a predetermined amount of liquid substance at predetermined locations in each mold portion. 24. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising annealing the solidified first casted layer by pouring molten metal to form a third and a fourth casted layers. 25. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein: the first casted layer is casted by pouring a first liquid substance having a first chemical composition; and the second casted layer is casted by pouring a second liquid substance having a second chemical composition. 26. The method according to claims 25, wherein the first liquid substance and the second liquid substance are selected from: two alloys of the same metallic element, two types of glass and two types of polymers. 27. The method according to claims 25 or claim 26, wherein the first liquid substance and the second liquid substance differ in at least one of: the amount and the type of additives, wherein the additives are configured to at least: evaporate and decompose during casting. 28. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: measuring a chemical composition of the liquid substance in the first container prior to pouring the liquid substance into at least one of: the first mold portion and the second mold portion; measuring the chemical composition of the corresponding casted layer; and comparing the measurements. 29. The method of claim 28, further comprising: removing the corresponding casted layer, if the measurements yield a difference in chemical composition larger than a threshold value; and pouring new liquid substance into the at least one of: the first mold portion and the second mold portion. 30. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein pouring the liquid substance into the second mold portion is in an amount sufficient to form the second casted layer and to compensate for at least one of: shrinkage of the first casted layer and thickness deviation in the first casted layer. 31. An additive casting apparatus comprising: a movable dispensing unit in fluid connection with a first container containing mold material, the dispensing unit comprising one or more liquid introduction ports for depositing the mold material; a movable pouring unit in fluid connection with at least one second container for holding liquid substance, the pouring unit comprising one or more liquid introduction ports for pouring at least one liquid substance; a build table for holding the deposited mold material and the poured liquid substance; and a controller configured to: 

 control the movable dispensing unit to deposit a first portion of a mold, layer by layer; control the movable pouring unit to pour at least one liquid substance into the first portion of the mold to form a first casted layer; control the movable dispensing unit to deposit a first portion of a mold, layer by layer, on top of the first portion; and control the movable pouring unit to pour the at least one liquid substance into the second portion of the mold to form a second casted layer on top of at least a portion of the first casted layer. 32. The additive casting apparatus of claim 31, further comprising: a joining unit configured to join the first and second casted layers prior to a deposition of a third mold portion and the pouring of a third casted layer. 33. The additive casting apparatus of claim 32, wherein the joining unit is at least one of: an induction heater, E-beam, a resistance welder, an arc welder, a laser welder, a torch, a gluing device, a cold fusion unit, a magnet for magnetic field flow, an ultrasonic bonding unit and a heater for diffusion bonding. 34. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one second container is a crucible and the liquid substance is one of: a molten metal and molten glass. 35. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of claims 31-33, wherein the at least one second container is a tank and the liquid substance is at least one of: a polymer resin or a molten polymer. 36. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a pre-heating unit for heating each casted layer prior to pouring an additional casted layer. 37. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: one or more surface treatment units for treating the surface of each casted layer after the solidification and prior to pouring an additional casted layer. 38. The additive casting apparatus of claim 26, wherein the one or more surface treatment units comprises at least one of: a machining device, a grinding device and a polishing device. 39. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: an enclosure filled with protective atmosphere for providing the protective atmosphere to the casted layers during casting. 40. The additive casting apparatus of claim 39, wherein the enclosure comprises a closed housing accommodating: the movable dispensing unit, the movable casting unit, the build table and a device configured to provide the protective atmosphere. 41. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the controller is further configured to: receive a three-dimensional (3D) part model of one or more solid parts, the 3D part model is divided into a plurality of casted layers; receive a 3D mold model of a mold, the mold model is divided into a plurality of mold portions, wherein the mold is designed to provide a desired shape to a liquid substance; control the deposition of the mold portions based on the mold model; and control the pouring of the casted layers based on the part model. 42. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the movable despising unit is configured to move in at least one axe. 43. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the movable pouring unit is configured to move in at least one axes. 44. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the build table is coupled to a movable platform. 45. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the movable pouring unit in fluid connection with two containers for holding a first liquid substances and a second liquid substances, and wherein the controller is configured to: control the movable pouring unit to pour the first liquid substance into the first portion of the mold to form a first casted layer; and control the movable pouring unit to pour the second liquid substance into the second portion of the mold to form a second casted layer. 46. An additive casting apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: at least one chemical composition sensor configured the measure at least of: the chemical composition of the liquid substance in the container and the chemical composition of the casted layers. 47. The additive casting apparatus of claim 45, wherein the at least one substance composition sensor based on X-ray or laser. 48. A casted metallic part comprising: at least a first casted layer comprising a first type of alloy; at least a second casted layer comprising a second type of alloy, joined to the at least first casted layer, wherein the first and second alloys are different alloys of the same metallic element. 49. The casted metallic part of claim 48, wherein the thickness of each casted layer is at least two orders of magnitude smaller than the perimeter of each casted layer. 50. The casted metallic part of claim 48 or claim 49, further comprising a third casted layer joined to at least one second casted layer. 51. The casted metallic part of 50, wherein the third casted layer comprising a third type of alloy of the same metallic element. 52. A casted metallic part according to any one of claims 48-51 , wherein the at least a first casted layer differs from the at least a second casted layer also in microstructure. 53. A casted metallic part comprising: at least a first casted layer having a first predetermined microstructure; at least a second casted layer having a second predetermined microstructure, joined to the at least first casted layer, wherein the first and second predetermined microstructures differ at least in the average grain size. 54. The casted metallic part of claim 53, wherein the thickness of each casted layer is at least two orders of magnitude smaller than the perimeter of each casted layer. 55. The casted metallic part of claim 53 or claim 54, further comprising a third casted layer joined to at least one second casted layer. 56. The casted metallic part of 55, wherein the third casted layer having a third predetermined microstructure.
